context.meal: breaded fish bites, roasted potatoes, tartar sauce
context.setting: Watching TV with TV in Front

episode_text:
day=23; meal=breaded fish bites, roasted potatoes, tartar sauce; setting=Watching TV with TV in Front; time_of_day=evening;
preferences: microwave_time=3 min; occlusion_relevance=minimize front occlusion; robot_speed=slow; skewering_axis=perpendicular to major axis; web_interface_confirmation=yes; transfer_mode=inside mouth transfer; outside_mouth_distance=not applicable; convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transfer=LED; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_feeding=autocontinue; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_drinking=autocontinue; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_wiping=autocontinue; convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transfer=LED; detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking=autocontinue; detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping=autocontinue; retract_between_bites=yes; bite_dipping_preference=do not dip; wait_before_autocontinue_seconds=1000 sec

microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF food is cold-served fruit or dessert AND affective state is neutral AND dining context is personal, social, or attention-directed THEN no microwave BECAUSE repeated cold-food meals were served without reheating, consistent with intended cold serving. IF food is a warm savory meal AND affective state is neutral AND dining context is personal, watching TV, or working on a laptop/tablet THEN 3 min BECAUSE repeated warm savory meals in personal and attention-directed contexts, including front-TV savory meals, often used thorough reheating, indicating a general preference for hotter savory food there. IF food is a warm savory meal AND affective state is neutral AND dining context is social with a person in front, left, or right THEN 2 min BECAUSE observed social savory meals repeatedly used moderate reheating, suggesting a stable social-setting preference for somewhat warm but not maximally hot food. IF food is a warm savory meal AND affective state is neutral AND dining context is personal THEN 2 min BECAUSE one personal evening savory meal also used moderate reheating, showing that personal savory meals can sometimes shift from 3 min to 2 min rather than always favoring maximum reheating. IF food is a mixed cold meal that includes dessert or fruit with sauce AND affective state is neutral AND dining context is watching TV to the right THEN 3 min BECAUSE the observed right-TV sweet mixed meal was reheated, indicating a context-specific exception where some sweet mixed meals are warmed.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF dining context is a quiet personal meal AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is any food THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE repeated personal meals did not prioritize view preservation. IF dining context includes a salient person or focal activity in front of the user AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is general meals or warm savory meals in front-attention or front-social contexts THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE repeated TV-in-front, laptop-in-front, and front-social meals selected front-occlusion minimization, and the new front-TV savory meal reinforces a stable preference to preserve the forward view when that view matters. IF dining context includes a focal activity or social partner to the right AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is any food THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE right-attention and right-social meals repeatedly selected right-occlusion minimization. IF dining context includes a focal activity or social partner to the left AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is any food THEN minimize left occlusion BECAUSE left-side attention meals and left-social meals prioritized preserving the left-side view. IF dining context is social with a person in front AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is cold fruit THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE the observed front-social fruit meal did not prioritize occlusion avoidance, showing that social context alone does not always override low-stakes food/context combinations.

robot_speed.user_tendencies: IF dining context is personal, social, or involves a concurrent visually attended activity AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is any food THEN slow BECAUSE the user selected slow speed across nearly all observed meals, including the new front-TV savory meal, indicating a generally comfort-first interaction style. IF dining context involves working on a laptop on the left AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is cold fruit THEN medium BECAUSE one observed left-laptop fruit meal used medium speed, suggesting a limited context-specific exception in a low-risk multitasking meal.

skewering_axis.user_tendencies: IF food requires fork acquisition AND affective state is neutral AND dining context is general, personal, social, TV-related, laptop-related, or includes fruit/dessert pieces THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE this option was selected across many observed meals, including the new front-TV fish-bites meal, favoring reliable bite acquisition. IF food is a warm savory item in strips, pieces, sandwich bites, nugget-like pieces, or bite-sized vegetables AND affective state is neutral AND dining context is personal, social with a person on the left, front, or right, watching TV to the right, watching TV in front, or working on a laptop on the left THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE repeated meals with solid savory bite-sized foods used parallel skewering, suggesting a narrower-bite preference for many savory solids. IF food is a warm savory bite-sized meal in a TV context AND affective state is neutral AND food condition includes fish bites or similarly soft breaded pieces THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE both observed fish-bite TV meals used perpendicular skewering, indicating that food properties can outweigh the broader bite-sized-savory tendency.

web_interface_confirmation.user_tendencies: IF dining context is a quiet personal meal AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is food requiring bite acquisition THEN no BECAUSE earlier personal savory meals often skipped confirmation, indicating a lower-interaction preference when alone. IF dining context is a quiet personal meal AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is a warm savory meal with fork-acquired solid pieces THEN yes BECAUSE one personal chicken-strip meal used explicit confirmation, indicating that even when alone the user may sometimes want verification for savory bite-acquisition tasks. IF dining context is social, including social meals with a person on the left, in front, or on the right, AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is any food requiring bite acquisition THEN yes BECAUSE observed social meals repeatedly used explicit confirmation, indicating a preference to verify bite acquisition when errors may be disruptive or visible. IF dining context involves watching TV in front or working on a laptop positioned to the left or in front of the user AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is any food requiring bite acquisition THEN yes BECAUSE repeated meals in these contexts often used explicit confirmation, and the new front-TV savory meal strengthens a broad preference for verification rather than skipping this step there. IF dining context involves working on a laptop to the left AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is a warm savory bite-sized meal such as nuggets with vegetables THEN no BECAUSE one left-laptop savory meal skipped confirmation, showing that this context does not uniformly require verification and that straightforward savory foods can favor reduced interaction burden. IF dining context involves working on a laptop to the right AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is any food requiring bite acquisition THEN no BECAUSE observed right-laptop meals skipped confirmation, suggesting a context-specific exception favoring reduced interaction burden there. IF dining context involves watching TV to the right or left AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is a cold fruit or sweet fruit/dessert meal THEN no BECAUSE observed side-TV low-stakes fruit or sweet meals skipped confirmation, indicating that in these attention-directed, lower-risk meals the user prefers less interaction. IF dining context involves watching TV in front AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is a warm savory bite-sized meal such as fish bites THEN yes BECAUSE the observed front-TV fish-bites meal used explicit confirmation, indicating that front-TV savory meals can favor verification rather than the lower-interaction pattern seen in some other front-TV meals. IF dining context involves watching TV to the right AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is a warm savory meal with bite acquisition such as fish bites and potatoes THEN yes BECAUSE the observed right-TV savory meal used explicit confirmation, indicating that right-TV savory meals can shift toward verification rather than the lower-interaction pattern seen for right-TV sweet or fruit meals.

transfer_mode.user_tendencies: IF dining context is any observed context AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is any food THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user cannot lean forward due to limited trunk control, and all observed meals used inside-mouth transfer.

outside_mouth_distance.user_tendencies: IF transfer_mode is inside mouth transfer THEN not applicable BECAUSE outside-mouth distance does not apply, and the user has consistently selected inside-mouth transfer.

convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context is a quiet personal meal AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is any task THEN speech + LED BECAUSE repeated personal meals used multimodal initiation cues, indicating a preference for a strong clear signal when alone. IF dining context is social with a person on the left, social with a person in front or right, or involves working on a laptop AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is any task THEN LED BECAUSE several meals in these socially or attentively constrained settings used quiet visual initiation cues. IF dining context involves watching TV in front of the user AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is a warm savory meal THEN LED BECAUSE the new front-TV savory meal used LED-only initiation, showing that front-TV meals do not always favor multimodal cues and that quieter visual signaling can be preferred during screen-focused savory meals. IF dining context involves watching TV in front of the user or watching TV to the left or right AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is screen-focused meals, including cold fruit and some warm savory meals, THEN speech + LED BECAUSE several observed screen-attention meals used multimodal initiation cues, suggesting that when the user is focused on a screen they often prefer a stronger ready signal. IF dining context is watching TV to the right AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is a warm savory meal THEN speech BECAUSE one observed right-TV savory meal used speech-only initiation, indicating a context-specific preference for an audible cue without LED in at least some right-TV savory settings.

detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfe.user_tendencies: IF dining context is personal or social, including social meals with a person on the left, in front, or on the right, AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is feeding THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user cannot reliably press buttons and repeatedly selected open-mouth readiness in personal and social meals, showing that low-effort initiation outweighed possible ambiguity from conversation in the observed data. IF dining context involves working on a laptop or watching TV, including front-, left-, or right-side attention contexts, AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is feeding THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected autocontinue in attention-divided contexts, including the new front-TV meal, indicating a stable preference to minimize interaction burden when occupied.

IF dining context is personal or social, including social meals with a person on the left, in front, or on the right, AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is drinking THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user cannot reliably press buttons and repeatedly selected open-mouth readiness for drink initiation in personal and social contexts. IF dining context involves working on a laptop or watching TV, including front-, left-, or right-side attention contexts, AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is drinking THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected autocontinue in attention-divided contexts, including the new front-TV meal, preferring lower-effort automation when occupied.

IF dining context is personal or social, including social meals with a person on the left, in front, or on the right, AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is mouth wiping THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user cannot reliably press buttons and repeatedly selected open-mouth readiness for wiping initiation in personal and social contexts. IF dining context involves working on a laptop or watching TV, including front-, left-, or right-side attention contexts, AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is mouth wiping THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected autocontinue in attention-divided contexts, including the new front-TV meal, consistent with preferring reduced interaction demand when occupied.

convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transf.user_tendencies: IF dining context is a quiet personal meal AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is any task THEN speech + LED BECAUSE repeated personal meals used multimodal completion cues, indicating a preference for a strong clear signal when alone. IF dining context is social with a person on the left, social with a person in front or right, or involves working on a laptop AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is any task THEN LED BECAUSE several meals in these settings used nonverbal completion cues. IF dining context involves watching TV in front of the user AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is a warm savory meal THEN LED BECAUSE the new front-TV savory meal used LED-only completion cues, showing that front-TV meals can favor quieter visual signaling at transfer completion. IF dining context involves watching TV in front of the user or watching TV to the left or right AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is screen-focused meals, including cold fruit and some warm savory meals, THEN speech + LED BECAUSE several observed screen-attention meals used multimodal completion cues, suggesting that when the user is focused on a screen they often prefer a stronger completion signal. IF dining context is watching TV to the right AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is a warm savory meal THEN speech BECAUSE one observed right-TV savory meal used speech-only completion cues, indicating a context-specific audible-cue preference in at least some right-TV savory settings.

detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding.user_tendencies: IF task is feeding AND affective state is neutral AND dining context is any observed context THEN perception BECAUSE the user selected sensor-based bite completion detection in all observed meals, and this avoids button presses the user cannot reliably perform.

detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking.user_tendencies: IF task is drinking AND affective state is neutral AND dining context is any observed context THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user cannot reliably press buttons or perform head gestures, and all observed meals used autocontinue.

detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping.user_tendencies: IF task is mouth wiping AND affective state is neutral AND dining context is any observed context THEN autocontinue BECAUSE the user cannot reliably press buttons or perform head gestures, and all observed meals used autocontinue.

retract_between_bites.user_tendencies: IF dining context is a quiet personal meal AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is any food THEN no BECAUSE repeated personal meals skipped retracting, indicating a preference for efficiency when no external view needs preserving. IF dining context includes a salient person or focal activity in front of the user AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is any food THEN yes BECAUSE the user repeatedly selected retracting between bites in front-attention contexts and front-social meals, and the new front-TV meal reinforces willingness to accept longer meal time to reduce prolonged visual obstruction when the attended forward view matters. IF dining context includes a focal activity or social partner to the right AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is any food THEN yes BECAUSE right-attention and right-social meals used retracting, showing the same preference when preserving the right-side view matters. IF dining context includes a focal activity or social partner to the left AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is any food THEN yes BECAUSE left-side attention meals and left-social meals also used retracting, extending this preference to left-side view preservation.

bite_dipping_preference.user_tendencies: IF food has no dip or sauce THEN do not dip BECAUSE dipping is not applicable. IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context is a quiet personal meal AND affective state is neutral THEN less BECAUSE the observed personal ranch-dressing meal used some dipping but not maximal dipping, indicating moderate sauce use when alone. IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context is social, including social meals with a person on the left or in front, AND affective state is neutral THEN less BECAUSE observed social meals with dip used less dipping, consistent with limiting messiness in social settings. IF food includes a dip or sauce AND dining context involves working on a laptop AND affective state is neutral THEN do not dip BECAUSE observed laptop meals with ketchup, sauce, or whipped cream used no dipping, showing a strong mess-avoidance preference when multitasking around equipment. IF food includes a sauce or dip AND dining context involves watching TV in front of the user AND affective state is neutral THEN do not dip BECAUSE the new front-TV savory meal with tartar sauce selected no dipping, extending the user's mess-avoidance tendency to front-TV attention contexts. IF food includes a sweet sauce AND dining context is watching TV to the right AND affective state is neutral THEN do not dip BECAUSE the observed right-TV sweet meal with sauce selected no dipping, extending the user's mess-avoidance tendency to that attention-directed context. IF food includes a savory dip or sauce AND dining context is watching TV to the right AND affective state is neutral THEN do not dip BECAUSE the observed right-TV fish-bites meal with tartar sauce also selected no dipping, strengthening the pattern that right-TV meals favor avoiding dipping even when sauce is available.

wait_before_autocontinue_seconds.user_tendencies: IF dining context is a quiet personal meal AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is any task using autocontinue THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE an earlier personal meal used the longest wait, indicating a preference for ample time before automatic progression when alone. IF dining context is a quiet personal meal AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is any task using autocontinue THEN 100 sec BECAUSE a later personal savory meal used the shorter wait, showing that personal meals do not always favor the longest delay and that faster progression is also acceptable there. IF dining context is social, including social meals with a person on the left, in front, or on the right, AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is any task using autocontinue THEN 100 sec BECAUSE the user selected a moderate wait in observed social meals, balancing time to respond with meal efficiency. IF dining context involves watching TV to the left AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is any task using autocontinue THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE the observed left-TV meal used the longest wait, aligning this context with other screen-attention settings where the user prefers extra time before automatic progression. IF dining context involves working on a laptop in front, working on a laptop to the left, working on a laptop to the right, or watching TV in front of the user AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is any task using autocontinue THEN 1000 sec BECAUSE repeated attention-divided meals in those contexts, including the new front-TV meal, used the longest wait, indicating a strong preference for extra time before automatic progression when attention is shared with another activity. IF dining context is watching TV to the right AND affective state is neutral AND food condition is any task using autocontinue THEN 100 sec BECAUSE one observed right-TV savory meal used the shorter wait, indicating a right-TV-specific preference for faster automatic progression than in other screen-attention contexts.
